definition: trough-to-crest height of an extremely high single maritime ocean surface water wave

definition_ml.en: trough-to-crest height of an extremely high single maritime ocean surface water wave

source_document: Faulkner D. (2001) Rogue waves – defining their characteristics for marine design. In: Olagnon M., Athanassoulis GA (eds) Rogue Waves 2000, Ifremer, France, pp 3-18
note: normally occurring far out at sea where physical factors such as high winds and strong currents cause waves to merge producing a single exceptionally large surface wave

note_ml.en: normally occurring far out at sea where physical factors such as high winds and strong currents cause waves to merge producing a single exceptionally large surface wave

remark: Not to be confused with seismic waves (Tsunamis).

remark_ml.en: Not to be confused with seismic waves (Tsunamis).
